task_completion.description: The draw.io application is open with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io (diagrams.net) interface is visible with the filename 'threat_model.drawio' displayed in the top bar or tab.

task_completion.description: The diagram includes shapes for External Entities, Processes, and Data Stores.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The canvas shows multiple distinct shapes representing entities (e.g., Mobile User, Admin), processes (e.g., API Gateway, Auth Service), and data stores (e.g., Credentials DB, Balance DB).

task_completion.description: The diagram includes Trust Boundaries and Data Flow arrows connecting the elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The canvas shows grouping boxes or dashed lines representing trust boundaries (e.g., DMZ, Internal Services) and directional arrows (edges) connecting the shapes to represent data flows.

task_completion.description: The diagram is annotated with the 6 STRIDE threats.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels or callouts on the diagram explicitly mention STRIDE threats: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege.

task_completion.description: A second page named 'Threat Summary' exists and contains a list or table of the threats.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page tab named 'Threat Summary' is visible at the bottom of the draw.io interface, and its canvas contains a text list or table summarizing the STRIDE threats.

task_completion.description: The completed diagram is exported as a PDF.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'threat_model.pdf' is visible in the file manager under the Diagrams folder, or a PDF viewer is open showing the exported diagram, or the draw.io PDF export dialog is seen completing.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the draw.io application interface (dragging shapes, typing text, adding arrows) rather than just writing an XML file via terminal or script.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the architecture specification file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window is visible showing the contents of 'mobile_banking_architecture.txt'.

integrity.description: The PDF was genuinely exported from the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using the File -> Export as -> PDF menu in draw.io, or the resulting PDF visually matches the created diagram.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The diagram must be saved at '/home/ga/Diagrams/threat_model.drawio' and exported to '/home/ga/Diagrams/threat_model.pdf'. The architecture requires exactly 19 shapes (4 external entities, 6 processes, 5 data stores, 4 trust boundaries) and 18 data flow edges. Required entities: Mobile User, Admin User, Payment Processor, Credit Bureau. Required processes: API Gateway, Authentication, Account Management, Transaction Engine, Notification, Fraud Detection. Required data stores: Credentials DB, Balance DB, Transaction Log, Audit Log, Session Cache. The diagram must be annotated with the 6 STRIDE threats (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege). The draw.io file must have at least 2 pages, with the second page named 'Threat Summary' containing a table or list of the threats.
